content:
Tutor Fee Guide South Africa 2026

What is a fair tutor fee in South Africa? This guide covers the typical fees charged by private tutors in 2026, broken down by subject, grade level, and region — so you know exactly what to expect and what represents fair value.

Typical Tutor Fee Per Hour (South Africa 2026)

Tutor TypeFee Per Hour
University student tutorR150–R250
Junior school tutor (Grades 1–7)R150–R300
High school tutor (Grades 8–10)R200–R350
Matric tutor Grade 11–12 (qualified)R300–R500
Subject specialist (Maths/Science)R350–R600
Matric specialist (exam prep focus)R400–R600
Online human tutorR200–R450
Group tutoring (per student)R100–R200

Tutor Fee by Subject

SubjectTypical Fee Range (per hour)
Mathematics (Grade 12)R300–R600
Physical Sciences (Grade 12)R300–R550
Accounting (Grade 12)R280–R500
English Home LanguageR200–R400
Life SciencesR250–R450
EconomicsR250–R450
Business StudiesR200–R400
AfrikaansR200–R380
Geography / HistoryR180–R350
University Maths / StatisticsR400–R800

Tutor Fee by Location

LocationFee Range (per hour)
Sandton / Rosebank / Bryanston (Joburg)R400–R600
Pretoria East / CenturionR350–R550
Cape Town (Southern Suburbs, Sea Point)R350–R550
Durban / UmhlangaR280–R500
Port Elizabeth / GqeberhaR220–R420
BloemfonteinR200–R380
Polokwane / NelspruitR180–R350
Small towns and rural areasR120–R280

Is the Tutor Fee Negotiable?

Yes — tutor fees are often negotiable, especially if you:

  • Book multiple sessions per week (bulk discount)
  • Commit to a term or semester (long-term discount)
  • Bring additional students to form a small group
  • Are willing to have sessions at the tutor's home or a library (saves travel time)

What Should Be Included in the Tutor Fee?

Before hiring a tutor, clarify what the fee includes:

  • Session preparation time: Some tutors charge extra for preparing customised materials
  • Travel/transport: Home visit tutors typically charge a travel supplement (R30–R150 extra)
  • Materials: Past papers, worksheets — some tutors provide these free, others charge extra
  • Cancellation policy: Many tutors charge for late cancellations (e.g., within 24 hours)

How to Find a Tutor at a Fair Price

  • University student tutors: Often the most affordable option. Find them through university SRC noticeboards, Facebook groups, or tutoring platforms
  • Online platforms: Superprof, Tuta-Me, LearnPick — compare multiple tutors and prices
  • School recommendations: Ask your teacher to recommend a tutor — they often know reliable, affordable options
  • WhatsApp community groups: Local community groups often have tutor recommendations

Affordable Alternative: AI Tutoring

For families who find tutor fees too expensive, AI tutoring offers exceptional value:

  • StudyBuddy is FREE to start — no credit card needed
  • R99/month for unlimited tutoring in ALL CAPS subjects (Grade 8–12)
  • Available 24/7 — midnight exam panic? No problem
  • Covers all subjects: Maths, Sciences, English, Accounting, Economics, and more

Compare: one in-person tutoring session (1.5 hours at R400/hour = R600) costs 6× the monthly StudyBuddy subscription — and that's just ONE session.

how much does a tutor cost
quickAnswer: Tutor fees in South Africa range from R150–R600 per hour in 2026, depending on the subject, grade, tutor's qualifications, and your location. Matric Maths/Science tutors in Johannesburg typically charge R350–R600/hour. For affordable tutoring, AI options like StudyBuddy cost just R99/month for unlimited access.

faqs.question: How much should I pay a tutor per hour in South Africa?
faqs.answer: A fair tutor fee in South Africa is R150–R250/hour for a university student tutor, R250–R450/hour for a qualified teacher or experienced tutor, and R400–R600/hour for a specialist matric tutor in high-demand subjects. Rates are higher in Johannesburg and Cape Town.

faqs.question: What is a reasonable tutor fee for Grade 12?
faqs.answer: For Grade 12 tutoring, a reasonable fee is R300–R500/hour for an experienced, qualified tutor. Less experienced university student tutors may charge R200–R300/hour. For Maths and Science, expect to pay towards the higher end of these ranges.

faqs.question: Is there a cheaper option than paying a tutor per hour?
faqs.answer: Yes. AI tutoring platforms like StudyBuddy offer unlimited help in all CAPS subjects for just R99/month — compared to R2,400–R6,000/month for regular in-person tutoring. Most students see similar academic improvement using AI tutoring consistently.
